questions.question: Explain the difference between programmed I/O and interrupt-driven I/O (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Programmed I/O requires CPU to continuously poll device status
Interrupt-driven I/O allows device to signal CPU when ready
Interrupt-driven I/O is more efficient for CPU utilization

questions.question: Describe the DMA data transfer process with its advantages and disadvantages (8 marks)
expectedPoints: CPU initializes DMA controller with transfer parameters
DMA controller takes bus control from CPU
DMA performs data transfer between device and memory
DMA releases bus and interrupts CPU when done
Advantage: frees CPU for other tasks during transfer
